claim: Resolution affix is appended at the end of the nowrap/ellipsis finding row while the row title tooltip still carries only fd.claim, so once the row truncates (routine at 11px for real claim + resolution lengths in a split tile) the resolution text, resolvedBy and resolvedAt are visually unreachable to sighted users; TEST-716 cannot catch this because it checks text and title combined
fix: Compose the affix string once and mirror it into the row title when shown (title = claim + affix), matching how gateTitle composes a full tooltip; propose CONV: a single-line nowrap/ellipsis row title tooltip MUST mirror every field the row renders inline (the full row text), so content clipped by the ellipsis stays reachable
resolution: Verified fixed: OrkyPane.tsx:237-242 composes the affix once and sets rowTitle = claim + full affix whenever the affix renders (non-affixed rows keep the bare claim); TEST-722 structurally rejects an unconditional title={fd.claim} and e2e TEST-723 re-run green (title carries claim + resolution + resolvedBy + formatted resolvedAt), 4/4 passed 2026-07-03

claim: resolvedBy renders as a bare unlabeled parenthetical directly after free-form resolution text (e.g. rewrote the guard (ux) at 2026-07-01 ...), indistinguishable from a parenthetical inside the resolution text itself and cryptic for lens-name values like (ux) or (quality)
fix: Label the attribution, e.g. 'by <resolvedBy>', mirroring the labeled 'at <ts>' pattern already used for resolvedAt on the same affix
resolution: Resolved by spec decision: amended REQ-110 (ESC-001 descent, human-sanctioned spec loopback) makes labeling the resolvedBy attribution an explicit MAY — 'optional polish, not required' (REQ-110(c)); the implementation keeps the unlabeled parenthetical by design and TEST-723 deliberately does not pin a label. Verified the affix renders consistently on both e2e vectors

claim: REQ-110 keys the affix on resolution !== null rather than non-empty, and REQ-109 maps the string verbatim (strOrNull, no trimming), so a resolved finding carrying resolution: '' renders an orphaned dangling label ('— resolution:' followed by nothing, or directly by the (resolvedBy)/at parts)
fix: Treat an empty-string resolution as no-affix at the display guard (resolution !== null && resolution !== '') or amend REQ-110 to a non-empty guard; keep the REQ-109 mapper verbatim
resolution: Verified fixed: display guard is now resolution !== null && resolution !== '' (OrkyPane.tsx:236) per amended REQ-110; e2e TEST-724 F-EMPTY vector (empty-string resolution with non-null resolvedBy/resolvedAt) re-run green — no dangling label, no orphaned resolvedBy/resolvedAt parts, row byte-identical to the null-resolution rendering

claim: Provenance pin records the wrong producer version: TEST-719 asserts CHANGELOG [Unreleased] contains '0.30.0' as 'the plugin version the fixtures were regenerated against', but the fixtures were verifiably produced by plugin 0.32.0 (commit 1c59d74, per the 04-tests.md TASK-103 record and Orky's git history — 0.30.0 landed 10:42, 0.32.0 landed 11:36, fixtures generated 12:09 on 2026-07-03). Nothing asserts the true version: deleting the CHANGELOG's '0.32.0' disclosure clause would leave the misleading 'targeting the spec's named plugin 0.30.0' claim green-guarded. The saving fact — gatekeeper/ is byte-identical between 3f0c016 (v0.30.0) and 1c59d74 (v0.32.0), so the emitted contract is identical — is asserted in prose but verified nowhere. Root cause: REQ-105(c) hardcoded '(0.30.0)' as the provenance datum at spec time instead of 'the version actually read from the producer at regeneration time'.
fix: Via tests-phase discipline (frozen suite): amend TEST-719's CHANGELOG assertion to require the actual regeneration-time producer version (toContain('0.32.0'), or a generic 'plugin <version> (commit <sha>)' shape) — keeping '0.30.0' only as the spec-time historical mention — and fix its it-description so 0.30.0 is not labeled the regeneration version. Optionally have generate-orky-contract-fixtures.mjs print/emit the producer's plugin.json version so the recorded value is read, not recalled. propose CONV: A provenance record for regenerated/bundled reference data (changelog, docs, test pins) MUST state the producer version actually read at generation time (e.g. from the producer's own version manifest), never a version literal copied forward from spec text.
resolution: TEST-719 amended (ESC-001/TASK-115): the toContain('0.30.0') pin is gone; the CHANGELOG assertion now requires the version-agnostic generation-time shape 'regenerated against plugin <semver> (commit <sha>)' which only the true producer record (0.32.0/1c59d74) satisfies and whose deletion turns the test RED; the it-description no longer labels 0.30.0 the regeneration version — verified by running the suite green

claim: CLAUDE.md:179 still describes Orky's 9-entry PHASE_ORDER as '(intake...human)' — a prose mirror of the v1 phase_order tail left unreconciled to v2 ('human-review'), violating REQ-107's MUST ('every prose mirror of the old v1 phase_order ... MUST be updated to v2 reality') and CONV-008's every-phrasing whole-tree discipline; the sentence now contradicts the very provenance caveat in docs/features/orky-status.md it points to (the spec's literal-array grep patterns structurally cannot catch this ellipsis phrasing)
fix: Update CLAUDE.md line 179 to '(intake...human-review)' or version-aware phrasing consistent with the reconciled provenance notes; re-run the CONV-008 scan including shorthand phrasings (e.g. rg 'intake.{1,3}human' filtered for non-human-review hits) across src docs CLAUDE.md .orky/baseline
resolution: CLAUDE.md:179 now reads '(intake…human-review)' (TASK-118); frozen TEST-720 encodes amended REQ-107 scan (4) over CLAUDE.md + both provenance sites and passes; independent rg 'intake.{1,5}human' over src docs CLAUDE.md .orky/baseline returns only the reconciled -review hit

claim: Regeneration provenance emphasis is inverted: the fixtures were actually regenerated against plugin 0.32.0 (04-tests.md TASK-103 execution record), but the CHANGELOG [Unreleased] entry leads with 'regenerated in one run ... targeting the spec's named plugin 0.30.0' and frozen TEST-719 asserts toContain('0.30.0') under the assertion message 'the plugin version the fixtures were regenerated against must be recorded' — the test pins the NOT-actually-used version as the provenance record and would fail any future correction that names only the true producer; the changelog's 0.32.0 note saves it from being false but the untested 'every golden assertion holds against either version' claim about 0.30.0 is unverifiable (0.30.0 no longer installed)
fix: Reword the CHANGELOG entry to lead with the actual producer version 0.32.0 (spec's 0.30.0 target as the parenthetical note), and amend TEST-719's CHANGELOG pin through the sanctioned test-change path to require the actual recorded producer version (or a version-agnostic 'regenerated against plugin <semver>' pattern) instead of the literal 0.30.0
resolution: CHANGELOG [Unreleased] now leads with 'regenerated against plugin 0.32.0 (commit 1c59d74) — the producer's version and commit as read at generation time' (TASK-119), 0.30.0 demoted to a concept-phase/spec-time historical mention; amended TEST-719 pins the version-agnostic shape instead of 0.30.0; the either-version claim is grounded in the byte-identical gatekeeper/ trees fact, re-verified myself (git diff 3f0c016..1c59d74 -- plugin/gatekeeper/ empty in C:/dev/Orky)

claim: New user-visible pane behavior is undocumented in its owning feature doc: docs/features/orky-pane.md — which owns OrkyPane rendering and the registry:detail payload — nowhere mentions the v2 '— resolution:' affix on resolved finding rows or OrkyFindingDetail's new resolution/resolvedBy/resolvedAt fields; only CHANGELOG [Unreleased] records them, and no REQ or TASK covers a feature-doc update (traceability gap: REQ-107's doc list names orky-status.md and orky-action-dispatch.md but omits the pane doc even though REQ-109/REQ-110 change the pane's payload and rendering)
fix: Document the finding_resolution display (case-insensitive resolved check, non-null resolution guard, resolvedBy/formatOrkyInstant resolvedAt inline) and the three additive OrkyFindingDetail fields in docs/features/orky-pane.md during doc-sync, and add the doc to the feature's traceability files
resolution: docs/features/orky-pane.md gained the 'v2 resolution display (feature 0015, REQ-109/REQ-110/REQ-113)' section (TASK-121) documenting all three OrkyFindingDetail fields incl. the verbatim/''-surviving mapping, both display guards, resolvedBy/resolvedAt surfacing and the row-title full-text mirror — verified accurate against OrkyPane.tsx and orky-root-detail.ts; traceability gap closed via new REQ-113 -> TASK-121 -> TEST-721 (green) and the doc listed in traceability.md's REQ-113 row

claim: REQ-105 MUST (fixtures regenerated against the installed plugin 0.30.0) and REQ-108 precondition (with plugin 0.30.0 installed) were violated: fixtures and the TEST-691 acceptance run used plugin 0.32.0 (commit 1c59d74) with no spec amendment - the 0.30.0 pin was already stale AT SPEC FREEZE (Orky shipped 0.31.0/0.32.0 at 11:18/11:36 local, before the 11:44 spec gate) - and frozen TEST-719 now pins the misleading 0.30.0 provenance string in CHANGELOG as the audited truth while the actual data source is 0.32.0
fix: Amend the spec to identify the producer by contract identity - the installed plugin reporting contract_version 2 (0.30.0 or later; actual at regeneration: 0.32.0 @1c59d74) - reword CHANGELOG so the ACTUAL source version leads the provenance record, and re-point TEST-719's 0.30.0 pin at the actual regeneration version. Verified mitigating fact for the resolver: plugin/gatekeeper/ is byte-identical between v0.30.0 and v0.32.0 (git diff 3f0c016..1c59d74 -- plugin/gatekeeper/ is empty), so no fixture data drifted. propose CONV: A spec/acceptance pin on an external producer must name the contract identity consumed (e.g. contract_version), recording the producer's release/commit as provenance - never a bare release number as the MUST.
resolution: Verified fixed at the root via ESC-001 Option A (Kevin, 2026-07-03, decision recorded in state.json): amended REQ-105/REQ-108 identify the producer by observable contract (contract_version 2 + committed contract.json byte-equal to live producer output) with the release/commit as a generation-time RECORDED FACT (0.32.0 @ 1c59d74 per the 04-tests.md TASK-103 record) and forbid any acceptance/test requiring a hardcoded version numeral; TEST-719 re-pinned to the version-agnostic shape 'regenerated against plugin <semver> (commit <sha>)' (passes); CHANGELOG [Unreleased] reworded so 0.32.0/1c59d74 leads the provenance record with 0.30.0 demoted to a historical spec-time mention

claim: 04-tests.md self-contradicts on TASK-103's actor and producer version: line 88 says 'regeneration is TASK-103, implementer, via the generator ONLY' and the TEST-711 row plus the Notes-for-the-implementer section say 'against plugin 0.30.0', contradicting the same file's appended tests-phase execution record (regenerated 2026-07-03 in the tests phase against installed plugin 0.32.0) and traceability.md's amended 'tests (test-designer) — moved by loopback amendment' row
fix: Mark the pre-amendment lines (04-tests.md:15, :88, :93-95) as superseded by the TASK-103 execution record (or correct actor to tests/test-designer and note the 0.32.0 actual), so the frozen artifact reads consistently for future auditors
resolution: 04-tests.md line ~102 reworded: the 'TASK-103, implementer, via the generator ONLY' phrasing is marked superseded by the amendment banner + TASK-103 execution record, both of which correctly name the tests-phase/test-designer actor and the 0.32.0/1c59d74 producer; verified no other actor-contradicting text remains in 04-tests.md, traceability.md, or traceability.json (all consistently say tests/test-designer for TASK-103).

claim: REQ-105 pins fixture provenance to a mutable external version numeral ('installed plugin 0.30.0') that drifted to 0.32.0 before regeneration; fixtures were actually produced by 0.32.0 (disclosed in 04-tests.md TASK-103 + CHANGELOG) yet TEST-719 freezes '0.30.0' as THE recorded provenance version — the primary recorded literal names a producer that never produced the committed bytes. Verified no data drift: live 0.32.0 'contract' output deep-equals the committed contract.json byte-order-identically.
fix: Treat the observable output contract (contract_version + live deep-equal, which REQ-105(c) already names) as the binding provenance pin and the producer version as informational-at-regeneration-time. propose CONV: a spec/test pinning the provenance of regenerated external-producer artifacts MUST pin the producer's observable output contract, never a frozen installed-version numeral that can drift between spec freeze and regeneration.
resolution: Amended REQ-105 (ESC-001) pins provenance by the observable output contract (contract_version: 2 + committed contract.json byte-equal to the live producer's output) with the producer release/commit a generation-time recorded fact, and forbids any assertion requiring a hardcoded version numeral; TEST-719 now asserts the version-agnostic shape — verified green via npx vitest run

claim: REQ-110's resolution details are commonly invisible in real UI: the affix is appended AFTER the claim on a nowrap/overflow-hidden/ellipsis row while the row tooltip stays fd.claim only, so any claim long enough to truncate clips the entire resolution affix (resolution, resolvedBy, resolvedAt) with no hover fallback; TEST-716 proves only DOM containment (toContainText/textContent ignore CSS clipping), so the spec's observable-in-text-or-title acceptance is met by a technicality the user never sees
fix: Surface the resolution details on the non-clipped surface too: for resolved rows set the row title to the claim plus the resolution affix text (or allow affixed rows to wrap), and add a visibility-aware pin. propose CONV: When an acceptance criterion requires data be observable on a row styled to truncate (nowrap/ellipsis), the data must also live on a non-clipped surface (e.g. the title attribute) and its test must not rely on textContent alone.
resolution: Verified fixed (same defect as FINDING-001): amended REQ-110 makes the non-clipped title surface a MUST — whenever the affix renders the row title mirrors claim + full affix (OrkyPane.tsx:242); e2e TEST-723 asserts the title carries claim, resolution text, resolvedBy and formatted resolvedAt (re-run green 4/4, 2026-07-03), so the observable-in-title acceptance is no longer satisfiable by a CSS-clipped technicality

claim: CLAUDE.md line 179 still describes Orky's PHASE_ORDER as '(intake...human)' — a stale pre-v2 fact REQ-107 required reconciled everywhere non-exempt (CLAUDE.md is explicitly named in REQ-107's CONV-023 grep scope, not on the exempt list). The regex sweeps TASK-109 ran ('doc-sync','human'], 'trailing human') don't match this compressed parenthetical, so it slipped through even though src/shared/orky-status.ts and docs/features/orky-status.md were correctly updated to human-review.
fix: Update the parenthetical to '(intake...human-review)' (or drop the trailing entry name) to match the reconciled provenance text in src/shared/orky-status.ts and docs/features/orky-status.md
resolution: CLAUDE.md:179's parenthetical now reads '(intake…human-review)' (TASK-118), consistent with the reconciled provenance text in src/shared/orky-status.ts and docs/features/orky-status.md; frozen TEST-720 encodes the compressed-parenthetical scan the original regex sweeps missed and passes, and my independent scan over src docs CLAUDE.md .orky/baseline returns only the -review hit

claim: REQ-105/TASK-103 required regenerating tests/fixtures/orky-contract/* against 'the installed plugin 0.30.0'; CHANGELOG.md's own provenance note (lines 229-231) discloses the plugin actually installed at regeneration time had already moved to 0.32.0, so the committed fixtures were regenerated against a different plugin build than the spec's MUST clause names. The shape happens to be identical so downstream assertions still hold, but the provenance record is internally inconsistent (claims '0.30.0' while admitting '0.32.0' in the same sentence) and the literal MUST was not met — this should have been a spec amendment/human check-in rather than a silently-absorbed deviation.
fix: Either re-run the generator against an actually-pinned 0.30.0 checkout to satisfy REQ-105 literally, or loop back to spec/human sign-off to amend REQ-105 to 'the actually-installed plugin at regeneration time' before treating the deviation as accepted
resolution: ESC-001 human spec loopback (Kevin, Option A) amended REQ-105 to identify the producer by observable contract (contract_version: 2, byte-equal live output) with 0.32.0/commit 1c59d74 recorded as the generation-time fact — the deviation is now spec-sanctioned via the required loopback, and the recorded-fact wording is consistent across 02-spec.md, CHANGELOG [Unreleased] and 04-tests.md's TASK-103 execution record (0.30.0 demoted everywhere to a spec-time historical mention; byte-identical gatekeeper/ trees re-verified in Orky's git)

claim: The design guarantees a recurrence of the exact failure that spawned this feature: TEST-691 hard-asserts live contractVersion === EXPECTED_CONTRACT_VERSION and note undefined, so the NEXT contract bump (v3) - or any lagging machine in a mixed fleet (a v1-era plugin fails ok:true, a pre-contract plugin fails the note-undefined assertion) - turns npm test red suite-wide for work unrelated to Orky, and the spec records no re-mirror playbook or skew-tolerant escape despite the plugin shipping three releases in one morning
fix: Either document the contract-bump playbook (a re-mirror feature checklist naming exactly the surfaces 0015 touched) in docs/features/orky-status.md, or make TEST-691 assert the handshake mechanics and loudly skip with the mismatch text when the live version differs from the pin - version-skew polarity is already fully pinned by the unit suite (TEST-708/TEST-709), so the hard equality adds fleet fragility, not coverage.

claim: The new case-insensitive 'resolved' check (fd.status.toLowerCase() === 'resolved') is inlined directly in OrkyPane.tsx's render map rather than extracted as a small named predicate (e.g. alongside isBlockingFinding in src/shared/orky-status.ts). It duplicates the case-fold idiom isBlockingFinding already uses for 'open' but isn't unit-testable in isolation — TEST-715 can only verify it via a source-text regex scan, not a real unit test of the predicate's truth table (mixed case, null, non-string). Low impact since it's used exactly once today, but the next consumer of finding-resolved-ness will either duplicate the regex again or need to extract it retroactively.
fix: Extract a small exported predicate (e.g. isResolvedFinding(status: string | null): boolean) near isBlockingFinding in src/shared/orky-status.ts, unit-test its truth table directly, and have OrkyPane.tsx call it instead of inlining the toLowerCase() comparison

claim: The doc's claim that the runtime handshake catches an in-place Orky upgrade the snapshot cannot is only true across app restarts: verifyOrkyContract caches per located path for the process lifetime (REQ-104 pins this byte-for-byte), so a mid-session in-place upgrade (Orky shipped 0.30 to 0.32 within 54 minutes on 2026-07-03) is invisible until restart; 0015 edited this very sentence (expected 1 to 2, TASK-108) without qualifying it
fix: Qualify the doc sentence: the handshake catches an in-place upgrade across app RESTARTS (the result is cached once per located path per process lifetime) - no code change, the binding decision keeps the cache.

claim: The sibling escalation row's resolved-decision affix — the very precedent amended REQ-110 cites — still has both defect classes the descent just fixed for finding rows: the row title stays esc.reason only, so on the nowrap/ellipsis row a long reason clips the entire '— decision: <decision>' affix with no hover fallback (escalation reasons/decisions are characteristically long — see ESC-001's own multi-sentence reason and decision), and the affix is guarded only on decision !== null, so an empty-string decision renders a dangling '— decision:' label. The descent leaves two adjacent rows in the same expanded feature behaving inconsistently for the identical clipping scenario
fix: Apply the finding-row pattern to the escalation row: guard on decision !== null && decision !== '', compose the affix once, and mirror reason + affix into the row title when the affix renders (the FINDING-001/003 fix shape); acceptable as a followups-doc deferral since it predates 0015, but the inconsistency should be recorded
